Alexander the Great Not So Great
TimelineThe Philip Factorvs NapoleonSources

Sources

Academic citations and references for further reading

Primary Sources

Arrian - Anabasis of Alexander

The most reliable ancient source on Alexander's campaigns

Arrian. Anabasis Alexandrou. Translated by P.A. Brunt. Harvard University Press, 1976.

Plutarch - Life of Alexander

Biographical account with moral commentary

Plutarch. Lives, Volume VII. Translated by Bernadotte Perrin. Harvard University Press, 1919.

Diodorus Siculus - Library of History

Universal history covering Alexander's reign

Diodorus Siculus. Library of History, Books 16-17. Translated by C. Bradford Welles. Harvard University Press, 1963.

Modern Scholarship

Philip II of Macedonia

Worthington, Ian

Yale University Press, 2008

Comprehensive study of Philip's military and political innovations

The Macedonian State

Hammond, N.G.L.

Oxford University Press, 1989

Definitive work on Macedonian institutions and military

Alexander of Macedon, 356-323 B.C.

Green, Peter

University of California Press, 1991

Critical biography questioning Alexander's traditional narrative

Conquest and Empire: The Reign of Alexander the Great

Bosworth, A.B.

Cambridge University Press, 1988

Analysis of Alexander's governance and administrative failures

Philip II and Macedonian Imperialism

Ellis, J.R.

Princeton University Press, 1976

Study of Philip's role in creating the Macedonian empire

The Generalship of Alexander the Great

Fuller, J.F.C.

Rutgers University Press, 1960

Military analysis showing Alexander's reliance on Philip's innovations

Napoleon Sources

The Campaigns of Napoleon

Chandler, David

Scribner, 1966

Comprehensive military history of Napoleon's campaigns

Napoleon: A Life

Roberts, Andrew

Viking, 2014

Modern biography emphasizing Napoleon's innovations

Academic Note

This educational resource is based on modern historical scholarship and primary sources. The conclusions presented represent the consensus of contemporary historians who emphasize Philip II's foundational role in Macedonian military success. The comparison with Napoleon is based on established military historiography.
